references.text: Craven, LA. 2005. Malesian and Australian Tournefortia transferred to Heliotropium and notes on delimitation of Boraginaceae. Blumea 50: 375-381
Frohlich, MW, M Thulin & MW Chase. 2020. Ninety-three new combinations in Euploca for species of Heliotropium section Orthostachys (Boraginaceae sensu APG). Phytotaxa 434: 13-21.
Hilger HH & N Diane. 2003. A systematic analysis of Heliotropiaceae (Boraginales) based on trnL and ITS1 sequence data. Botanische Jahrbücher 125: 19-51
Johnston, IM. 1951. Studies in the Boraginaceae. Journal of Arnold Arboretum 32: 1-26; 99-122.
Luebert, F, G Brokamp, J Wen, M Weigend & HH Hilger. 2011. Phylogenetic relationships and morphological diversity in Neotropical Heliotropium (Heliotropiaceae). Taxon 60: 663-680
Riedl, H. 1997. Boraginaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 13: 43-144

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 29 May 2020

species.citation: (R.Br.) M.W.Frohl. & M.W.Chase , Phytotaxa 434 (2020) 15; ≡ Heliotropium bracteatum R.Br. , Prodr. Fl. Nov. Holland. (1810) 493 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 377.
species.distribution: Australia, Java, Pacific Ocean. LUZON: Ilocos Norte, MINDANAO: Davao.

species.citation: (Willd.) Diane & Hilger , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 125 (2003) 49; ≡ Heliotropium strigosum Willd. , Sp. Pl., 4th Ed. 1 (1797) 743 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 378. = Heliotropium brevifolium Wall. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 2 (1824) 2 ; Riedl, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 102.
species.distribution: Africa, Bangladesh, Bhutan, China, India, Laos, Middle East, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Cagayan, Ilocos Norte (photos), NCR, Zambales.
species.notes: Open sandy places near the sea.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 30 March 2025

species.citation: (Blanco) Mabb. , Mabberley's Pl.-Book, 4th Ed. (2017) 1101; ≡ Tournefortia arborea Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 129. = Heliotropium foertherianum Diane & Hilger , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 125 (2003) 46. = Tournefortia argentea L.f. , Suppl. Pl. (1782) 133 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 376; Riedl, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 117; Romeroso et al., Asian J. Biol. Life Sc. 10 (2021) 437; ≡ Argusia argentea (L.f.) Heine , Fl. Nouv.-Calédonie & Dépend. 7 (1976) 108 ; Dayang Awa in Tree Fl. Sabah & Sarawak 2 (1996) 95; ≡ Messerschmidia argentea (L.f.) I.M.Johnst. , J. Arnold Arbor. 16 (1935) 164 ; J. Arnold Arb. 32 (1951) 121; Type: from Sri Lanka.
species.distribution: Africa, Andaman Isls, Australia, Borneo, China, Indian Ocean, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madagascar, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Vietnam. BALABAC, BATANES, BORACAY (photos), BUYAYAO (photos), DANJUGAN (photos), DIGYO ISL (photos), HOMONHON, LUMBUCAN, LUZON: Aurora (photos), MANTIGUE, MINDANAO: Misamis Oriental (photos), MINILOC, PALAWAN, SIBUYAN (photos), SULU ARCHIPELAGO, TURTLE IS.

species.citation: Craven , Blumea 50 (2005) 379; ≡ Tournefortia tetrandra Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 845 ; I.M.Johnst., J. Arnold Arb. 16 (1935) 150; Riedl, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 123.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sumatra.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 130 ; Robinson, PJS 4c (1909) 695; Riedl, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 103.
L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 130 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 395; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 377; I.M.Johnst., J. Arnold Arb. 32 (1951) 111; Riedl, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 105.
species.notes: Now pantropical. A common weed in the settled areas throughout the Philippines at low elevation.

species.citation: (I.M.Johnst.) Craven , Blumea 50 (2005) 379; ≡ Tournefortia luzonica I.M.Johnst. , J. Arnold Arbor. 16 (1935) 156 ; Riedl, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 118; Type: Adduru 237 (A, K), Luzon. ssp. luzonicum. Distribution: New Guinea, Philippines. LUZON. Notes: In mountainous regions. Native. ssp. angustissimum (Riedl) Craven, Blumea 50 (2005) 379; ≡ Tournefortia luzonica ssp. angustissima Riedl, Blumea 41 (1996) 443; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 119; Type: Ridsdale 1477 (L, holo), Luzon: Zambales, Sta Cruz, Acoje Mine concession area. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Zambales. Notes: On ultramafic soil. Only known from type locality. Native. ssp. sublucens (I.M.Johnst.) Craven, Blumea 50 (2005) 379; ≡ Tournefortia luzonica ssp. sublucens (I.M.Johnst.) Riedl, Blumea 41 (1996) 443; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 119; Type: BS 44553 Ramos & Edaño (A, BM, K), Luzon: Zambales prov. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Zambales. Native.
species.distribution: LUZON: Benguet, Cagayan, Ilocos Sur, Zambales, MINDANAO: South Cotabato, MINDORO.

species.citation: (I.M.Johnst.) Craven , Blumea 50 (2005) 380; ≡ Tournefortia muelleri I.M.Johnst. , J. Arnold Arbor. 16 (1935) 157 ; Riedl, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 120; Type: from Australia.
species.distribution: Australia, ?Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, New Guinea, Philippines. CATANDUANES, ?LUZON: ?Cagayan, ?NEGROS.
species.notes: Along rivers and in tidal areas.

species.citation: (Lam.) Craven , Blumea 50 (2005) 380; ≡ Tournefortia sarmentosa Lam. , Tabl. Encycl. 1 (1792) 416 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 394; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 377; I.M.Johnst., J. Arnold Arb. 16 (1935) 147; J. Arnold Arb. 32 (1951) 115; Riedl, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 121. = Tournefortia horsfieldii Miq. , Fl. Ned. Ind. 2 (1858) 927 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 376.
species.distribution: Australia, Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan. BALABAC, BASILAN, BILIRAN, LUBANG, LUZON: Aurora, Bataan, Benguet, Cagayan, Cavite, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, La Union, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Pampanga, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Zambales, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao del Sur, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos), POLILLO, PUJADA, TAWI-TAWI, UBIAN.
